explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Equinix executives' discussions reference the development of a service for securely tracking data lineage. These discussions also anticipate a new role for data quality sign-off, indicating a policy for AI data governance.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This article reports on Equinix executives' discussions regarding AI ethics and development, providing evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports governance by mentioning Equinix's development of a service for "securely tracking data lineage" and anticipating a new role for data quality sign-off, indicating a policy for AI data governance. The source also touches on privacy by discussing the importance of knowing data set lineage to address bias, such as in voice-to-text for non-native speakers, highlighting awareness of privacy-related challenges in AI.
relevant_sources.title: ETC Executive Coffee: Equinix Ponders Consequences of AI

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: Equinix executives' discussions reference the importance of knowing data set lineage to address bias. These discussions also highlight awareness of privacy-related challenges in AI.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

relevant_sources.summary: This article reports on Equinix executives' discussions regarding AI ethics and development, providing evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports governance by mentioning Equinix's development of a service for "securely tracking data lineage" and anticipating a new role for data quality sign-off, indicating a policy for AI data governance. The source also touches on privacy by discussing the importance of knowing data set lineage to address bias, such as in voice-to-text for non-native speakers, highlighting awareness of privacy-related challenges in AI.
relevant_sources.title: ETC Executive Coffee: Equinix Ponders Consequences of AI

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Equinix's published materials discuss the development of a service for securely tracking data lineage, an aspect of governance. This pillar is one of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars with documented evidence, with governance also anticipating a new role for data quality sign-off. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars, based on a review of 12 publicly available sources.
